change_summary: Shadow-audited volatility-contraction breakout candidates across three fixed OHLCV windows without changing production strategy code.
hypothesis: A volatility-contraction breakout shadow entry may surface institutional accumulation candidates with useful forward returns and limited same-day A/B overlap, distinct from gap/opening-range continuation and add-on trigger families.
notes: Artifact: docs/experiments/artifacts/exp_20260426_volatility_contraction_breakout_shadow.json. Candidate counts were 22/215/46 across late_strong/mid_weak/old_thin. Same-day A/B overlap was 18.18%/12.56%/26.09%. 10d forward return was unstable: late_strong -1.60% with 35.00% win rate, mid_weak +3.22% with 69.30% win rate, old_thin +2.82% with 67.39% win rate. Decision: observed_only; promising in two weaker windows but not production-ready because the dominant recent window is negative and no production-path replay was run.

locked_variables: volatility contraction breakout shadow entry definition
parameters.single_causal_variable: volatility contraction breakout shadow entry pattern
